title: How to SUMIF with a wildcard (sum rows that start with / contain text)
task: Total the numbers whose label matches a pattern, e.g. every category starting with 'North'.

excel.note: * = any characters; ? = one character. Use *text* for 'contains'.

libreoffice.note: Works when Tools>Options>Calc>Calculate has wildcards enabled (default for xlsx).

explanation: The asterisk wildcard matches any trailing text, so 'North*' catches 'North East', 'North West' and 'Northland' (10+7+2 = 19) but not 'South' or 'East'. Use '*North*' to match North anywhere in the text.
